description_long: This is a large, commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage and a classic cylindrical body. The airplane features four underwing engines, two on each side, mounted on swept-back, low-mounted wings. The tail section is equipped with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a standard tail design. The landing gear is visible and consists of a tricycle configuration with two main gear units and a nose gear. The exterior is primarily white with blue and gray striping, and the livery includes the name 'AEROFLOT' and a flag on the tail. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows indicating a multi-seat, enclosed configuration.
description: A large, white commercial jet airliner with four underwing engines, swept low-mounted wings, and a conventional tail. The fuselage is marked with 'AEROFLOT' and a flag, and the cockpit is forward-mounted with multiple windows.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, four underwing engines, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the length of the fuselage, the forward cockpit, and the tricycle landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, including the vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the engine arrangement under the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage and wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing jet engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows.
